questionHtml:
What is the reason for antibiotics not damaging human cells?

A. The dose is too small to be harmful.

B. The dose is taken for only a short time.

C. Antibiotics occur naturally in humans.

D. Human metabolism is different from bacterial metabolism.

meta.title: What is the reason for antibiotics not damaging human cells? A. The dose is too small to be harmful. B. The dose is taken for only a short time. C. Antibiotics occur naturally in h
breadcrumbLabels: C3.2.13. Antibiotics as chemicals that block processes occurring in bacteria but not in eukaryotic cells
